Russell Wilson and Ciara lifestyle



How will Russell Wilson make money after retirement?







With a heavy heart, the “former” word will now be added before “NFL quarterback” for the Seattle Seahawks legend Russell Wilson…Just yesterday, he posted a heart-humbling goodbye to football. And now we are in tears!The former New York Giants signal-caller called it quits after 14 long seasons. Over the years, his estimated net worth ranged from $165 million to $185 million.His multi-talented wife, Ciara , has an estimated net worth of $20 million. Their combined net worth is around $185 million to $205 million. Russell Wilson and Ciara are still the most talked-about NFL power couple because their love story -- spanning over a decade -- still looks and sounds like a teenage dream!This love has taken them far in life; today, they are major endorsers of several cross-industry brands across sports, tech, beauty, fashion, and telecommunications.Together, they are the creators of The House of LR&C and the Why Not You Foundation. Let’s learn more about their money dynamics.According to Spotrac, Russell Wilson’s total career earnings through 2026 are over $315 million.He has played for four NFL teams in 14 years: Seattle Seahawks [10], Denver Broncos [2], Pittsburgh Steelers [1], and New York Giants [1].There have been two major contract transactions: a four-year, $140 million contract with Seattle in 2019 and a five-year, $242 million contract with Denver in 2022.In 2024, the Broncos paid Wilson $39 million to leave after cutting him. Pittsburgh grabbed the bargain-priced one-year value of $1.21 million and made Denver pay $37.79 million in 2024.In 2025, he signed a one-year, $10.5 million contract with the New York Giants. But since he stayed on the bench more than he played on the gridiron…he probably decided it was time.According to Spotrac’s calculated market value, if Wilson had decided to play in 2026 for his 15th NFL season, he was projected to sign a one-year, $5.7 million contract.But he probably got a better deal with CBS Sports, where he will take up Matt Ryan’s role.His total career earnings are $315 million, as per Spotrac, and his net worth is estimated to be $185 million, according to Celebrity Net Worth.According to Realtor, the couple relocated to the East Coast in 2024 when he signed a one-year contract with the Pittsburgh Steelers.Then, in Dec. 2025, the New York Post reported that the couple was eyeing a Gilded Age mansion on the Upper West Side of New York City.The residence served as headquarters for the House of Free Russia from the 1940s to the 1990s.Randall Jackson purchased the Gilded Age mansion for $1.4 million in 1999. It was put on sale in Jan. 2013 for $50 million. In April 2025, it was listed again for $29.75 million.This house hunt happened shortly after Wilson signed a one-year, $10 million contract with the New York Giants in 2025.The exact physical address of their New York home has not been disclosed by the couple. However, Ciara spoke about New York last year.Ciara’s paternal side is from New York. “It’s in my genes. It’s in my DNA,” Ciara said on the Today Show in July 2025.“New York is awesome. It’s so much culture. It’s so much fun. So much diversity. I’m excited for our kids to go to school in New York.My dad’s actually from New York. My dad’s whole side of his family is from New York as well.”Ciara wrote an emotional comment on Russell Wilson’s retirement post: “The best in the world! There’s only one, 3! Always so proud of you!It’s a true blessing to go on this journey with you! My greatest inspiration! I love you so much! [GOAT emoji and Heart Hands emoji].”NFL Insider Adam Schefter reported on June 1 that Wilson is finalizing a deal to become a CBS Sports analyst. He will join CBS’ Sunday pregame show, “The NFL Today.”The show features host James Brown, former NFL wide receiver Nate Burleson, and former Pittsburgh Steelers head coach Bill Cowher.The position was formerly filled by Matt Ryan. Ryan is currently the President of Football Operations for the Atlanta Falcons. His CBS Sports salary is not publicly disclosed.However, former NFL quarterback Tony Romo’s salary at CBS Sports was revealed. As per the New York Post, Tony Romo’s salary with CBS is roughly $17 million per season.The former Dallas Cowboys quarterback’s final season was 2016. He joined CBS Sports in 2017. He signed a massive extension in 2020 for at least five seasons.The deal is believed to be over $100 million. This makes Tony Romo the highest-paid NFL analyst in TV history.Tom Brady reportedly earns $37.5 million per year working for FOX Sports, having signed a massive 10-year, $375 million contract.In contrast, Russell Wilson’s representatives have not revealed the details of his contract with CBS Sports.
Article Summary: Russell Wilson and Ciara's combined net worth in 2026 is estimated at $185 million to $205 million. Wilson is a 10-time Pro Bowler and a three-time MTV Video Music Awards winner. Ciara is a multi-talented singer who has sold more than 45 million records.

Article Title: Holly Holm makes candid Ronda Rousey admission while revisiting one of UFC’s biggest rivalries
Article Text:
Holly Holm and Ronda Rousey (Image Via Getty)

The Ariel Helwani Show

Holly Holm says a rematch with Ronda Rousey has always been available

Up & Adams







A fresh round of discussion has started between former UFC rivals Holly Holm and Ronda Rousey . The conversation picked up after an older interview clip of Rousey began circulating online again. In the video, Rousey was asked about facing Holm in another fight and confidently said she would beat her. The comment quickly caught the attention of MMA fans, bringing one of the sport’s biggest rivalries back into the spotlight.Not long after, Holm shared her thoughts during an appearance on. While responding to the comment, the former UFC champion said she has always been willing to fight Rousey again. However, she also suggested that Rousey has never shown real interest in a rematch since their famous meeting in 2015. Even though both fighters have moved in different directions, the topic continues to interest fans more than a decade later.The latest discussion started after Rousey appeared onwith Kay Adams. During the interview, she was asked what would happen if she fought Holm again. Rousey answered confidently and said she would defeat her former opponent.At the same time, she also made it clear that she is retired, making another fight unlikely.Speaking to Ariel Helwani, Holm responded by saying that she has never closed the door on a second fight. She explained that her position has remained the same since their first meeting ended.According to MMA Fighting’s Damon Martin, Holm said: “I highly doubt she’ll ever want a rematch. I always have said since the minute the last fight was over, I’ll always rematch her. That’s always been available. But she wanted to come back and have this win and go back and enjoy and have a win like that. No hate from me. I hope she does well. I hope she does whatever she wants with her life. That’s her life. I’d always be open to fight her again. ”The rivalry dates back to November 2015 at UFC 193. On that night, Holm shocked the MMA world by knocking out Rousey and capturing the UFC women’s bantamweight title. It was the first loss of Rousey’s professional MMA career after she had started with a perfect 12-0 record.Since then, both fighters have taken different paths. Rousey later lost to Amanda Nunes in her final UFC appearance in December 2016. Holm, meanwhile, continued competing and recently challenged Stephanie Han for the WBA female lightweight boxing title on May 30. Han kept the belt through a majority decision, although Holm finished the fight with a significant advantage in landed strikes.While another fight between Holm and Rousey appears unlikely, the rivalry still remains one of the most talked-about stories in women’s combat sports.
Article Summary: Holly Holm says a rematch with Ronda Rousey has always been available. Former UFC champion says she 'highly doubt' Rousey will ever want a rematch. Rousey recently said she would beat Holm in a rematch if they fought again.

Article Title: NHL trade rumor: Toronto Maple Leafs could explore a low-risk move for Patrik Laine this offseason
Article Text:
Patrik Laine appeared only in a handful of games during the 2025-26 season because of injury issues, raising questions about his durability (Credit: Getty Images)

Toronto Maple Leafs linked to low-risk gamble on former 40-goal scorer Patrik Laine







The Toronto Maple Leafs could explore a low-risk move this offseason, with reports suggesting that the club is planning to move former 40-goal scorer Patrik Laine as free agency approaches. Reports are suggesting that the Maple Leafs could emerge as a surprise landing spot for winger Patrik Laine when NHL free agency opens on July 1.As Laine is expected to leave the Montreal Canadiens after his contract expires, speculation is growing that Toronto may view the former 40-goal scorer as a low-risk gamble during a crucial offseason. According to reports, his last contract was 4 years, worth of $34.8 million with a cap hit of $8.7 million per season, and it expired at the end of the 2025-26 season.The Toronto Maple Leafs are planning to explore a low-risk and highly-rewarding move this offseason, with reports linking that they are approaching 40-goal scorer Patrik Laine as free agency approaches.According to recent speculation highlighted by The Big Lead, Toronto may consider taking a chance on Laine if the veteran winger reaches the open market on July 1. Reports suggest that the idea has stemmed from previous discussions between the Toronto Maple Leafs and the Montreal Canadiens involving Laine before the trade deadline, though no deal has materialized so far.Meanwhile, Adam Wylde suggested that Laine’s injury reserve could create a unique contract opportunity for interested teams, “If you can sign 28, 29-year-old Patrick Laine and his base salary is a million, but it could maybe go up to five if he scores 40 goals and plays 60 games, I think a lot of teams would take that chance. It’s a good opportunity to go get some cheap goal scoring. If I’m Chayka, I’d do it,” he added.Previously, the 28-year-old player’s career has been marked by setbacks. He appeared in only a handful of games during the 2025-26 season due to his injury issues, raising questions about his durability and long-term fit.But, as the Maple Leafs' quest for offensive depth continues in order to reshape their roster under new management, a low-cost deal for Patrik Laine would benefit the team much more than a major financial commitment.It is to be noted that the Maple Leafs finished 32-36-14 and missed the playoffs for the first time in nearly a decade, triggering major organizational changes and an aggressive roster retool, which is why they are in search of a head coach and good players as well who can bring back the lost glory.
Article Summary: Toronto Maple Leafs linked to low-risk gamble on former 40-goal scorer Patrik Laine. Reports suggest that the Maple Leafs could emerge as a surprise landing spot for winger when NHL free agency opens on July 1. Laine is expected to leave the Montreal Canadiens after his contract expires. The 28-year-old player’s career has been marked by setbacks.

Article Title: Elliotte Friedman reports Detroit Red Wings captain Dylan Larkin requests a trade out of Michigan
Article Text:
Dylan Larkin has been the face of the Red Wings for several seasons and remains one of the organization's most important players (Image via Getty Images)

Dylan Larkin requests a trade out of Michigan following the club’s offseason drought







The Detroit Red Wings are about to face a big blow after captain Dylan Larkin's future with the franchise remains uncertain this season. Reports suggest that the player has requested a trade out of the team and is looking for new possibilities. The Red Wings have repeatedly fallen short with their performance, as they have failed to qualify for the postseason, and this has been ongoing for quite long. In this season, they finished 41-31-10 in the Eastern Conference. Reports suggest that the club's inability to return to the postseason and its direction under Steve Yzerman contributed to the captain's decision for a fresh start. Dylan Larkin has been one of the most influential faces of the Red Wings for several seasons, and he continues to be one of the most important players. However, speculations suggest that the Michigan native has reportedly decided to move out of the Detroit Red Wings due to their inability to return to the postseason. This has fueled league-wide speculation regarding the captain's long-term future with the team.According to reports by insider Elliotte Friedman, the star forward has recently requested a trade out of Michigan. The insider stated, “There’s a lot to unpack here.First, should mention that no one would comment. Not Larkin, not agent Pat Brisson, not Red Wings Executive Vice-President & GM Steve Yzerman.”He further added, “Second, Larkin’s involvement in the trade market is massive. He has a full no-trade clause both this season and next, so his control over the situation does not change July 1. He’s a top-line centre in a league desperate for centres, so there’s going to be a ton of interest. His play at the 4 Nations and Olympics was stellar. That’s going to excite potential trade partners.”The Michigan native has consistently produced offensively while serving as Detroit's leader both on and off the ice. He also played a significant role for Team USA during its successful run at the 2026 Winter Olympics, displaying an impressive performance for all.Unfortunately, the franchise's playoff drought and return to postseason contention remains one of the longest in the NHL. This is what perhaps led the captain to take this decision.The 29-year-old recorded 34 goals and 67 points in 74 games last season while continuing to serve as Detroit's most important forward. However, any potential deal would be complicated as Larkin has a full no-trade clause, which gives him significant control over his future.
Article Summary: Dylan Larkin has reportedly requested a trade out of the Detroit Red Wings. The 29-year-old has been one of the most influential faces of the Red Wings for several seasons. Reports suggest that the club's inability to return to the postseason and its direction under Steve Yzerman contributed to the captain's decision.

Article Title: Taylor Swift just surpassed Kim Kardashian’s net worth as her wedding to Travis Kelce draws closer
Article Text:
(Getty)

Taylor Swift is worth $2 billion: the most powerful WAG in sports just got a Forbes update



The Life of a Showgirl

The Kardashian comparison nobody asked for but everyone needed



The Life of a Showgirl







Taylor Swift, yes, the same woman spotted in Kelce's suite, the same woman who turned NFL Sundays into a cultural event, the same woman the internet spent two years arguing was "too present" at games, just hit a $2 billion net worth. Forbes made it official in March 2026, and the number has been circulating again this week for good reason.Two. Billion. Dollars.Not two billion in combined assets with a partner. Not two billion in brand valuation. Two billion dollars, personally, in her name, built from the ground up, starting with a guitar and a notebook in Nashville.Here's what makes this genuinely unhinged in the best way. Swift is the first person to reach this level of wealth primarily through songwriting and performing, not a beauty brand, not a shapewear line, not a reality show. Just music.The Eras Tour became the highest-grossing concert tour of all time. Then in May 2025, she bought back the masters to her first six albums for $360 million, meaning every future royalty cheque goes straight into her pocket. Her October 2025 album,sold four million copies in its first week, the biggest debut in music history, and that was basically the final push over the line.Her music catalog alone is valued at roughly $900 million. Real estate adds another $100 million across Nashville, LA, New York, and Rhode Island.The woman owns zip codes. Multiple. Simultaneously.Swift's $2 billion now officially exceeds Kim Kardashian's estimated $1.9 billion, which means Travis Kelce's girlfriend is richer than the woman who built an empire selling body tape and contour kits. Kim built Skims. Taylor built a catalog. Both are billionaires. Only one of them just lapped the other.For context, Kelce himself is worth an estimated $70 million. His girlfriend has him beat by $1.93 billion. The most powerful person in that relationship has always been obvious; now Forbes agrees.Swift shows no signs of slowing down. With her masters now back in her hands andstill generating royalties, the $2 billion figure is likely a floor, not a ceiling. Every stream, every sync deal, every stadium ticket from here on out flows directly back to her.And for everyone who spent 2023 rolling their eyes at Taylor Swift showing up to Arrowhead every Sunday, the woman in that suite was already worth more than most NFL franchises could dream of paying their entire roster. She wasn't there for clout. She was there because she wanted to be. There's a difference. A $2 billion difference, to be exact.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift is worth $2 billion, according to Forbes. She is the first person to reach this level of wealth primarily through songwriting and performing. Her music catalog alone is valued at roughly $900 million. She bought back the masters to her first six albums for $360 million.

Article Title: Serena Williams shares first look at Queen’s Club while long awaited comeback at 44 draws closer
Article Text:
Serena Williams shares first look at Queen’s Club while long awaited comeback at 44 draws closer (Image via Getty)



Serena Williams and Victoria Mboko begin Queen’s Club partnership as tennis stars react to comeback







Serena Williams is officially back on a tennis court, and fans are already excited about what comes next. The 23-time Grand Slam champion was seen at Queen’s Club in London on Thursday as she prepared for her first tournament since stepping away from professional tennis in 2022. The 44-year-old American shocked the sports world last week when she announced her return to competition after nearly four years away from the tour.Williams will play women’s doubles at next week’s HSBC Championships at Queen’s Club. She has received a wildcard entry and will team up with Canadian teenager Victoria Mboko. Their appearance could also be the first step toward a possible return to Wimbledon, which begins on June 29.Before practice, Williams and Mboko posed for photos on the grass courts. Mboko later shared her excitement on social media, writing: “The Queen is back. An honor to share the court with one of the greatest athletes of all time this week. Even more excited to play doubles together! Tennis is pretty special.”The return marks a new chapter for Williams, who has not played a competitive match since losing in the third round of the 2022 US Open.At that time, she avoided using the word retirement and instead said she was “evolving away” from tennis.When she announced her comeback last week, Williams shared a video of herself on court while notifications appeared on her phone. The clip included the message: “Guess everybody heard the news.”In a statement released through tournament organizers, Williams said: “Queen’s Club feels like the perfect place to begin this next chapter.”She added: “Grass has given me some of the most meaningful moments of my career, and I’m excited to be back competing on one of the sport’s most iconic stages.”The comeback has already drawn reactions from some of the biggest names in tennis. Speaking at the French Open, Naomi Osaka said: “I don’t really care about tennis. I think it’s good for me. I’ll be very entertained.”Osaka continued: “I think it will bring people to watch tennis, which she always does bring an audience with her. So I think it’s good.”She also added: “I’m going to be tuned into the first match, for sure. I think a lot of people are. Everyone knows Serena and Venus were my role models growing up, so it’s going to be cool to see her on the grounds again.”Meanwhile, Coco Gauff said: “I did say one of my biggest regrets was not being able to play her.”Gauff added: “Everybody has been hearing rumours and stuff. So, if they are true, I guess we’ll find out in a couple days based off the rumours. We’ll see, but I think it would be cool for this sport to have a legend back playing.”
Article Summary: Serena Williams and Victoria Mboko begin partnership at Queen's Club. Williams will play women's doubles at next week's HSBC Championships. She has received a wildcard entry and will team up with Canadian teenager. Williams has not played a competitive match since losing in the third round of the 2022 US Open.

Article Title: Australian tennis player James McCabe breaks silence on incident that left his father hospitalized
Article Text:
James McCabe (Image Via Getty)

James McCabe calls for witnesses to come forward after alleged incident involving his father Patrick McCabe







Australian tennis player James McCabe has spoken out after an incident at the Birmingham Open that he says left his father and coach, Patrick McCabe, needing hospital treatment. The 22-year-old claimed a tournament staff member acted aggressively before an alleged physical confrontation took place near a doorway at the event. According to McCabe, the incident happened while he was preparing to warm up for a match ahead of Wimbledon qualifying events.The allegations have quickly drawn attention because they involve a player, his coach, and a member of tournament staff. Speaking to The First Serve, McCabe said his father was later taken to hospital and is now dealing with increased pain and other symptoms. He has also questioned why he has not been given access to footage from the venue. At the same time, police have confirmed they are investigating a report of an alleged assault. No arrests have been made, and inquiries remain ongoing.Speaking to The First Serve, James McCabe said the situation began when a staff member allegedly started shouting and behaving aggressively at the Birmingham Open. He claimed the individual later followed him and his father before the alleged incident occurred.“My father says the staff member physically put his hands on him near a doorway,” McCabe said.“My understanding is that the staff member admitted to the police that he put his hands on my father, although the venue disputes the characterisation of assault.”McCabe has since made a public appeal for anyone who witnessed the incident to come forward. He said several adults were present at the time and believes their accounts could help clarify what happened.“The key issue now is that there were adult witnesses present, including adult supervisors,” McCabe said.“I am asking for those adult witness accounts to be properly identified, preserved and independently reviewed.”The Australian player also shared details about his father’s health. According to McCabe, Patrick has previously undergone C5 cervical spine surgery. He said the alleged incident has worsened existing medical problems.“My dad has had previous C5 cervical spine surgery and is now in worsening pain with arm symptoms, pins and needles, shooting pain, dizziness and severe pain that stopped him sleeping last night,” he said.Meanwhile, West Midlands Police confirmed that officers are looking into the matter.“(Officials have) received a report of a man being assaulted at a venue in Sir Harrys Road, Birmingham,” the police statement said.“No arrests have been made and enquiries are ongoing.”As the investigation continues, questions remain about exactly what happened and whether additional witness accounts or venue footage will provide further answers.
Article Summary: Australian tennis player James McCabe has spoken out after an incident at the Birmingham Open that he says left his father and coach, Patrick McCabe, needing hospital treatment. McCabe claims a tournament staff member acted aggressively before an alleged physical confrontation took place near a doorway at the event. McCabe has since made a public appeal for anyone who witnessed the incident to come forward. West Midlands Police confirmed they are investigating a report of an alleged assault.

Article Title: Shakira admits she never knew her own strength until Piqué walked out of her life: "Life is a bi*** but worth living"
Article Text:
Shakira opens up on resilience, motherhood and life after Gerard Piqué (Getty Images)

How did Shakira discover a new sense of strength after her public struggles?

Why does Shakira credit motherhood for helping her through difficult times?







For Shakira , the past few years have been defined as much by personal hardship as professional triumph. The Colombian superstar has faced a public breakup, a prolonged legal dispute in Spain and relentless media attention, yet she now finds herself entering one of the strongest phases of her career. Speaking candidly about that journey, the 49-year-old singer reflected on how adversity reshaped her understanding of herself, revealing that the challenges she endured ultimately exposed a strength she never knew she had.Shakira's comments arrive at a moment when her career is once again reaching new heights. While preparing for a packed summer that includes major tour dates and a historic FIFA World Cup final halftime performance, she looked back on the period that followed her split from former Barcelona defender Gerard Piqué.The breakup in 2022 became one of the most scrutinized celebrity separations in recent memory. At the same time, Shakira was navigating a lengthy tax dispute in Spain that kept her in headlines for reasons far removed from music.Rather than allowing those events to define her, she used them as a source of growth.“I always thought that I was more fragile or weaker than what life proved me to be,” Shakira said in a recent interview.Reflecting on difficult moments, she added, “Through those challenging moments, I discovered how resilient we all are. And I truly admire so many women that just have to push forward every day, push forward through hardship or through whatever life throws at them.”The singer believes every setback carries value, even when the lessons arrive through pain. “Behind every experience in life, there’s always a lesson, and we have to be grateful for all those lessons, even for the people that leave you with scars,” she said. “Because they just make us better.”That mindset has fueled a remarkable professional resurgence. Her music connected deeply with listeners during a period of personal upheaval, helping launch a new chapter built on honesty and self-expression.While her music became an outlet, Shakira says motherhood remained the foundation that kept her moving forward. The singer shares sons Milan and Sasha with Piqué, and she described parenthood as a transformative force during her toughest days.“I think motherhood makes you strong,” said Shakira. “I feel that the moment I became a mother, I saw life through a different perspective. They are true teachers. I learned so much from them every day."She recalled the reality of balancing emotional challenges with everyday responsibilities.“When your world collapses and you have to get up and pay the bills, and still provide for your family, and still prepare breakfast, and take your kids to school—life needs to go on because you have the responsibility to watch after two human beings. Seeing my kids also overcome those difficulties, that also fills my heart.”Her reflections come shortly after a favorable ruling involving Spain's tax authorities and ahead of a busy stretch on the Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour. Another major milestone awaits on July 19, when she joins Madonna and BTS for the first-ever FIFA World Cup final halftime show at MetLife Stadium.For Shakira, the upcoming moment feels symbolic. Soccer has played a defining role in both her personal and professional life, from the global success of “Waka Waka” to the relationship that began during the 2010 World Cup. Yet her focus today is broader than past chapters.“Life is beautiful in spite of all the pain that we all go through. It is what it is,” she said. “Like they say: ‘Life is a bitch,’ but it's so worth living it, you know?”
Article Summary: Shakira opened up about how adversity reshaped her understanding of herself. The Colombian singer says she discovered how resilient we all are. She credits motherhood for helping her through difficult times. Her comments arrive at a moment when her career is once again reaching new heights.

Article Title: Anna Kalinskaya shares surprising reason her boyfriend walked out during tense French Open battle
Article Text:
Anna Kalinskaya shares surprising reason her boyfriend walked out during tense French Open battle (Image via Getty)



Anna Kalinskaya explains how Bella’s barking affected her French Open match against Anastasia Potapova









Anna Kalinskaya had a memorable run at the French Open, but one unexpected moment during her fourth-round match quickly became a talking point. The Russian tennis star revealed that her boyfriend had to leave the stadium with their dog Bella after the pet barked during an important point of the match. The incident happened during Anna Kalinskaya’s fourth-round win over fellow Russian player Anastasia Potapova at Roland Garros. Speaking to Tennis Channel after the match, Kalinskaya said Bella’s barking distracted her at a key moment and even affected one of her shots. To avoid further interruptions, her boyfriend took the dog out of the stadium.Dogs have become a familiar sight at this year’s French Open. Tournament organizers allowed several players to bring their pets to Roland Garros as a way to provide comfort and emotional support during the two-week Grand Slam event. According to French Open officials, 10 dogs received special accreditations to enter the grounds.While talking to Tennis Channel, Anna Kalinskaya shared exactly what happened during the match. “She could not watch because she barked in the first set, that was why my match was not very joyful. “She actually barked and I missed the ball into the net. It was actually quite an important point. But then my boyfriend left the stadium with her.”Despite the distraction, Kalinskaya stayed focused and battled through a tough match. She defeated Anastasia Potapova 6-4, 2-6, 7-6 to reach her first French Open quarterfinal. After the win, she jokingly said the tournament should continue offering its special dog-walking service for players.Her impressive run ended in the quarterfinals, where Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska beat her 7-6, 6-3.The French Open had already made it clear that dogs were welcome only if they behaved properly. Speaking earlier in the tournament, players’ support and relations manager Kildine Chevalier explained that pets could enter players’ areas and even sit courtside in players’ boxes, but barking was not acceptable.“The dog accreditations are a bit of fun, it’s real but not real, it’s a great souvenir,” Chevalier said. “The players have loved it. “Dogs have gone into the players’ box courtside and that’s not ideal. It is fine if they do not bark.”Several other players also brought their dogs to Roland Garros, including world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka, Anastasia Potapova, Marta Kostyuk and Belgian player Zizou Bergs.
Article Summary: Russian tennis star Anna Kalinskaya's boyfriend had to leave the stadium with their dog Bella after the pet barked during an important point of the match. The incident happened during her fourth-round win over fellow Russian player Anastasia Potapova at Roland Garros. French Open had already made it clear that dogs were welcome only if they behaved properly.

Article Title: Microsoft HR head Amy Coleman to employees in memo: I want to be transparent about how things are feeling across Microsoft and what ...
Article Text:
Feedback from managers and leaders

Message from Amy Coleman

Read the complete memo from Microsoft HR head Amy Colemanhere



My goal is to have an ongoing, open conversation with you about what it's like to work at Microsoft. Today's post is part of that, sharing what we're seeing in our H2 Employee Signals results so we can have a dialogue about what's most important to you.

Your day-to-day experience matters, and so much of it is shaped by your manager and leader. At the same time, I want to be transparent about how things are feeling across Microsoft - what's working, where we're making progress, and where we still have more to do.

Thank you to the 71% of you who took part in Employee Signals and shared nearly 265,000 comments. It helps us better understand where we are as a company right now.

What the data is telling us

Our Thriving score, feeling energized and empowered while doing meaningful work, rose 3 points to 79, with consistent progress across every element.

Top strengths: A sharp focus on addressing security challenges, feeling included on teams, and teams working in ways that reflect our culture. When asked what helps you do your best work, comments pointed to empowerment, teamwork, culture, communication, and customer focus.





Top opportunities: Expanding experiences in role, making sure you have what you need to be productive in today's work environment, and more clearly connecting your work to broader org objectives. Comments surfaced challenges around strategy, communication, processes, customer focus, and speed of execution.





While much of this feedback is encouraging, I also know we are in a time of intense and exciting change. Many of you shared feelings of uncertainty and pressure as the work evolves. The leadership team and I hear that, and we're committed to being more transparent, communicating more frequently, and giving context wherever we can.

Manager and Leader Signals

In April, many of you leveraged our annual Manager and Leader Signals survey to share feedback with your manager and skip-level leader to help them grow.

Highlights include:

Confidence in your manager remained strong at 85.

The top strength for our managers is embracing new challenges to drive innovative solutions. At the same time, one of our biggest opportunities is strengthening how managers coach and support others through their day-to-day challenges.

Nearly 368,000 comments recognized strengths in clarity, empowerment, and driving results, while also pointing to opportunities around transparency, communication, and career development.

Thanks again for sharing your feedback.





Microsoft’s cheif poeple officer Amy Coleman has infromed the employees that the company is committed to greater transperancy as staff navigate ‘intense and exciting change.’ In an internal memo viewed by Business Insider, Coleman outlined both encouraging signals and areas of concern from Microsoft’s latest employee surveys. According to the twice-yearly Employee Signals survey, the Microsoft employees reported feeling more “energized” and “empowered” to do meaningful work. The ‘Thriving score’ of the company rose three points to 79. Along with this, the emlpiyees also highlighted the strengths such as prioritizing security challenges (88% favorable), feeling included on teams (86%), and teams reflecting Microsoft’s culture (86%). But the survey also revealed some of the challenges. Employees also expressed lower satisfaction with oppurtuntities to broaden experience (79%), productivity support in today’s work environment (80%), and clarity on how their work connects to organizational objectives (81%).In a different Manager and Leader Signals survey, confidence in managers remained strong at 85%. Staff praised managers for embracing new challenges (86%) and creating inclusive environments (86%).However, coaching, feedback, and motivation scored lower: only 76% felt managers coached them effectively (down 5 points), while clear feedback (79%) and motivation (82%) also declined.In the internal memo, Coleman has acknowleged the mixed results and wrote, “While much of this feedback is encouraging, I also know we are in a time of intense and exciting change. Many of you shared feelings of uncertainty and pressure as the work evolves. The leadership team and I hear that, and we’re committed to being more transparent, communicating more frequently, and giving context wherever we can.”Additionally, she also thanked 71% of employees who participated, noting nearly 265,000 comments were submitted. Coleman emphasized that day‑to‑day experiences are shaped by managers and leaders, and pledged to keep an “ongoing, open conversation” about workplace culture and expectations.
Article Summary: Microsoft’s cheif poeple officer Amy Coleman has infromed the employees that the company is committed to greater transperancy as staff navigate ‘intense and exciting change’ In an internal memo viewed by Business Insider, Coleman outlined both encouraging signals and areas of concern. According to the twice- yearly Employee Signals survey, the Microsoft employees reported feeling more “energized” and “empowered” to do meaningful work.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.
